fix: remove machine-specific runtime paths (#412)

* fix(mcs): move AG-44→72 route registration inside create_app()

Problem
_register_extra_routes() was called at module level (line 2844) after
create_app() returned. Every caller that constructed a fresh app via
create_app() — tests, WSGI, hot-reload — received only the 66 base
routes. AG-44→72 (sovereign_operator, event_bus, chains, decision_queue,
multi_agent_contract, governed_inference, and 20+ others) were structurally
unreachable on any fresh app instance.

Fix
Split `return FastAPI(routes=[...])` into an assignment `_app = FastAPI(...)`,
call `_register_extra_routes(_app)` immediately before `return _app`, and
remove the now-redundant module-level call. Registration is guaranteed for
every create_app() invocation regardless of call site.

Proof
- Fresh create_app(): 176 unique routes (178 raw), up from 66
- AG-44→72 families all present: sovereign_operator (3), event_bus (3),
  chains (4), decision_queue (9), multi_agent_contract (3), governed_inference (3)
- /api/qs_runs 200, /api/chains/latest 200, /api/sovereign_operator/latest 200
- MODULE_ROUTES == FRESH_ROUTES (178 == 178) — no double-registration
- /health 200, /api/operator_status 200, /api/decisions/latest 200 — no regression

* AG-P9-HV2: Harden telegram_send credential resolution — remove IC_NOTIFY fallback

Violation fixed: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN_IC_NOTIFY was second in the fallback chain.
IC_NOTIFY token is invalid (401). A non-empty invalid token in an `or` chain does
NOT fall through — it gets used and fails. This masked the credential problem.

Rule enforced: no silent fallback that converts failure → success.
Credential source is now deterministic: explicit payload > GGMESH env var only.
Bad/missing GGMESH → hard error {"ok": False, "error": "bot_token_missing"}.

Proofs: V-P9G (deterministic source), V-P9H (full auto_plan trace), V-P9I (diff justified)

commit.message:
Fix web print dialog not triggering (#142)

* Fix web print dialog not triggering after window.open

window.open with noopener disconnects the returned reference, making
document.write a no-op on the new window. Removed noopener (safe since
we write our own content, not navigating to untrusted URL), null out
opener after writing, and use setTimeout instead of onload which
doesn't fire after document.write/close.

Fixed in both print-checklist preview and settings self-PR export.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix false 'Export failed' alert when print popup works

printWindow.opener = null can throw a DOMException in some browsers,
which triggers the catch block showing 'Export failed' even though the
popup opened successfully. Wrapped opener nullification and print()
calls in their own try/catch blocks to prevent these from aborting
the flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Extract shared webPrint helper, null opener before writing content

- Move duplicated window.open/print logic into src/utils/webPrint.ts
- Named constant WEB_PRINT_DELAY_MS replaces magic number 250
- Null out opener immediately after window.open, before document.write,
  to prevent reverse-tabnabbing even if HTML contains scripts
- Both print-checklist preview and settings export now use the helper

commit.message:
Formula audit: fix OCV polynomial, battery endurance, GIQE, ISA density

Researched and verified all 17 model formulas against credible sources.
Fixed 4 issues found:

1. LiPo OCV polynomial (battery.py): Old 3rd-order gave 3.525V at 50% SoC
   (too low — real LiPo plateau is ~3.6-3.7V). Replaced with 5th-order fit
   calibrated to typical LiPo discharge curves: 3.0V→3.62V→4.2V.
   Refs: Plett "Battery Management Systems" 2015, Chen & Rincon-Mora IEEE 2006

2. Battery endurance formula (battery.py): Units bug — energy_Wh was divided
   by (power_W / 60) giving Wh/(W/min) = wrong units. Fixed to
   (energy_Wh / power_W) * 60 = correct minutes.

3. GIQE coefficients (image_quality.py): C3 was -0.334 (typo), corrected to
   -0.344 per Leachtenauer et al. 1997 / Applied Optics 47(5) 2008. Also
   fixed GSD units: GIQE 4 requires inches, code was passing meters.

4. Envelope solver ISA density (envelope_solver.py): cruise_power_est used
   hardcoded rho=1.225 (sea level). Now computes ISA density at actual
   altitude using standard tropospheric formula.

Also updated UKF Jacobian to match new 5th-order OCV derivative.

Verified formulas against sources:
- ISA density exponent 4.2561 = g/(R·L)-1 ✓ (ICAO Doc 7488)
- Induced drag Cdi = CL²/(π·AR·e) ✓ (Anderson, Fund. of Aerodynamics)
- Rotor thrust T = Ct·ρ·n²·D⁴ ✓ (standard dimensional analysis)
- GSD = (Sw·h)/(fl·px) ✓ (photogrammetry standard, ODM reference)
- Dryden turbulence MIL-F-8785C scale lengths ✓
- BSFC part-throttle 15% penalty ✓ (conservative but reasonable)
- Altitude derating 1.5-3%/1000ft ✓ (standard NA engine)

All 35 tests pass.
